running, 5 pending), 20 deployments, 15 services. Git is 3 commits behind main. ArgoCD app is Healthy and Synced.\",\n    \"urgency\": \"normal\"\n}\n\n\ndef _wrap_in_fence(payload: dict, fence_type: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Wrap a payload in different fence formats.\"\"\"\n    body = json.dumps(payload)\n    if fence_type == \"json\":\n        return f\"```json\\n{body}\\n```\"\n    elif fence_type == \"plain\":\n        return f\"```\\n{body}\\n```\"\n    elif fence_type == \"bare\":\n        return body\n    else:\n        raise ValueError(f\"Unknown fence type: {fence_type}\")\n\n\n# ============================================================================\n# Parsing Implementations\n# ============================================================================\n\ndef manual_fence_strip_current(text: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Manual fence stripping as used in router.py and synthesize.py.\n\n    This is the CURRENT implementation in those files (lines 251-254 in router.py,\n    lines 143-147 in synthesize.py).\n    \"\"\"\n    raw = text.strip()\n \"\"\"Manual fence stripping using regex pattern.\"\"\"\n    text = text.strip()\n    match = _FENCE_PATTERN.match(text)\n    if match:\n        return match.group(1)\n    return text\n\n\ndef centralized_parse_llm(text: str, expect_json: bool = True) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"\n    Full parsing using centralized response_parser utilities.\n\n    This uses strip_markdown_fences() + any fence stripping.\"\"\"\n    return json.loads(text)\n\n\n# ============================================================================\n# Benchmark Runner\n# ============================================================================\n\ndef benchmark_operation(\n    operation: Callable,\n    text: str,\n    iterations: int = 1000,\n) -> BenchmarkResult:\n    \"\"\"\n    Benchmark a single operation.\n\n    Args:\n        operation: Callable that takes text and returns parsed result\n        text: Input text to parse\n        iterations: Number of times to run the operation\n\n    Returns:\n        BenchmarkResult with timing statistics\n    \"\"\"\n    times = []\n\n    # Warmup run (not measured)\n    try:\n        operation(text)\n    except Exception:\n        pass  # Ignore warmup errors\n\n    for _ in range(iterations):\n        start = time.perf_counter()\n        try:\n            result = operation(text)\n            _ = result  # Use result to avoid optimization\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f\"  ERROR: {e}\")\n            continue\n        end = time.perf_counter()\n        times.append((end - start) * 1000)  # Convert to ms\n\n    if not times:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"All iterations failed\")\n\n    total = sum(times)\n    avg = statistics.mean(times)\n    median = statistics.median(times)\n    min_t = min(times)\n    max_t = max(times)\n    ops_per_sec = iterations / (total / 1000)\n\n    operation_name = operation.__name__\n\n    return BenchmarkResult(\n        operation=operation_name,\n        total_time_ms=total,\n        avg_time_ms=avg,\n        min_time_ms=min_t,\n        max_time_ms=max_t,\n        median_ms=median,\n        ops_per_sec=ops_per_sec,\n configurations\n    payloads = [\n        (\"small\", _SMALL_PAYLOAD),\n        (\"medium\", _MEDIUM_PAYLOAD),\n        (\"large\", _LARGE_PAYLOAD),\n    ]\n\n    fence_types = [\"json\", \"plain\", \"bare\"]\n\n    operations = [\n        (\"Current Manual (router/synth)\", manual_fence_strip_current),\n        (\"Manual with Regex\", manual_fence_strip_regex),\n        (\"Centralized strip_markdown_fences\", strip_markdown_fences),\n        (\"Full parse_llm_response\", lambda t: centralized_parse_llm(t, expect_json=True)),\n        (\"Direct json.loads (no fence)\", direct_json_loads),\n    ]\n\n    # Run benchmarks\n    for payload_name, payload in payloads:\n        print(f\"\\n{'=' * 80}\")\n        print(f\"PAYLOAD: {payload_name.upper()} (~{len(json.dumps(payload))} bytes)\")\n        print(f\"{'=' * 80}\")\n\n        for fence_type in fence_types:\n            fenced_text = _wrap_in_fence(payload, fence_type)\n\n            print(f\"\\n--- Fence format: {fence_type.upper()} ---\")\n            print(f\"{'Operation':<40} | {'Avg (ms)':<10} | {'Median (ms)':<12} | {'Min/Max (ms)':<15} | {'Ops/sec':<12}\")\n            print(\"-\" * 100)\n\n            for op_name, op_func in operations:\n                # Skip direct json.loads for fenced payloads (will fail)\n                if fence_type != \"bare\" and op_name == \"Direct json.loads (no fence)\":\n                    continue\n\n                try:\n                    result = benchmark_operation(op_func, fenced_text, iterations)\n                    all_results.append((payload_name, fence_type, result))\n\n                    print(\n                        f\"{result.operation:<40} | \"\n                        f\"{result.avg_time_ms:<10.4f} | \"\n                        f\"{result.median_ms:<12.4f} | \"\n                        f\"{result.min_time_ms:.4f}/{result.max_time_ms:.4f} | \"\n                        f\"{result.ops_per_sec:<12.0f}\"\n                    )\n                except Exception as e:\n                    print(f\"{op_name:<40} | ERROR: {e}\")\n\n    return all_results\n\n\ndef print_summary(all_results):\n    \"\"\"Print summary of results with recommendations.\"\"\"\n\n    print(\"\\n\" + \"=\" * 80)\n    print(\"SUMMARY & RECOMMENDATIONS\")\n    print(\"=\" * 80)\n\n    # Group by operation type\n    by_operation = {}\n    for payload_name, fence_type, result in all_results:\n        key = result.operation\n        if key not in by_operation:\n            by_operation[key] = []\n        by_operation[key].append(result.avg_time_ms)\n\n    print(\"\\nAverage latency across all payloads and fence formats:\")\n    print(f\"{'Operation':<40} | {'Avg (ms)':<10} | {'Relative to slowest':<20}\")\n    print(\"-\" * 80)\n\n    averages = {op: sum(times) / len(times) for op, times in by_operation.items()}\n    max_avg = max(averages.values())\n\n    for op, avg in sorted(averages.items(), key=lambda x: x[1]):\n        relative = avg / max_avg if max_avg > 0 else 0\n        print(f\"{op:<40} | {avg:<10.4f} | {relative:<20.2%}\")\n\n    print(\"\\n\" + \"=\" * 80)\n    print(\"KEY FINDINGS\")\n    print(\"=\" * 80)\n\n    # Find fastest and slowest\n    fastest_op = min(averages.items(), key=lambda x: x[1])\n    slowest_op = max(averages.items(), key=lambda x: x[1])\n\n    print(f\"✓ Fastest: {fastest_op[0]} ({fastest_op[1]:.4f} ms avg)\")\n    print(f\"✗ Slowest: {slowest_op[0]} ({slowest_op[1]:.4f} ms avg)\")\n    print(f\"✓ Speedup: {slowest_op[1] / fastest_op[1]:.2f}x\")\n\n    # Compare current implementation vs alternatives\n    if \"Current Manual (router/synth)\" in averages:\n        current_avg = averages[\"Current Manual (router/synth)\"]\n\n        print(f\"\\nCurrent manual implementation (router.py, synthesize.py): {current_avg:.4f} ms\")\n\n        if \"Manual with Regex\" in averages:\n            regex_avg = averages[\"Manual with Regex\"]\n            speedup = current_avg / regex_avg\n            if speedup > 1:\n                print(f\"  → Regex pattern is {speedup:.2f}x FASTER than current\")\n            else:\n                print(f\"  → Regex pattern is {1/speedup:.2f}x SLOWER than current\")\n\n        if \"Centralized strip_markdown_fences\" in averages:\n            centralized_avg = averages[\"Centralized strip_markdown_fences\"]\n            speedup = current_avg / centralized_avg\n            if speedup > 1:\n                print(f\"  → Centralized strip_markdown_fences is {speedup:.2f}x FASTER\")\n            else:\n                print(f\"  → Centralized strip_markdown_fences is {1/speedup:.2f}x SLOWER\")\n\n        if \"Full parse_llm_response\" in averages:\n            full_avg = averages[\"Full parse_llm_response\"]\n            speedup = current_avg / full_avg\n            if speedup > 1:\n                print(f\"  → Full parse_llm_response is {speedup:.2f}x FASTER\")\n            else:\n                print(f\"  → Full parse_llm_response is {1/speedup:.2f}x SLOWER\")\n\n\ndef main():\n    \"\"\"Main benchmark ZAI proxy response unwrapping (removes outer \"result\" field)\n6\t2. Markdown code fence stripping (```json, ```, etc.)\n7\t3. JSON parsing with clear error messages\n8\t4. Graceful fallback for malformed responses\n9\t\n10\tThis utility is used across:\n11\t- Intent Router (src/intent/router.py)\n12\t- Synthesize Strand (src/synthesize/strand.py)\n13\t- Self-Modification Agent (src/agents/self_modification.py)\n14\t- Any other LLM interaction points\n15\t\n16\t## Error Handling Patterns\n17\t\n18\tWhen ParseLLMError is raised, choose recovery strategy based on context:\n19\t\n20\t### Corrective Retry Pattern (Router-style)\n21\tUse when:\n22\t- LLM call is early in pipeline (cheap to retry)\n23\t- Failure cascades to downstream operations\n24\t- Retrying doesn't require re-running expensive operations\n25\t\n26\tExample: Intent router retries once because malformed response affects all downstream processing.\n27\t\n28\tSee: src/intent/router.py classify_utterance()\n29\t\n30\t### Fallback Result Pattern (Synthesize-style)\n31\tUse when:\n32\t- LLM call is AFTER expensive operations (fetch, DB queries, etc.)\n33\t- Data already obtained should not be discarded\n34\t- User should see partial results in degraded-state UX\n35\t\n36\tExample: Synthesize strand returns fallback result because fetch operations already completed.\n37\t\n38\tSee: src/synthesize/strand.py synthesize()\n39\t\n40\t### Documentation\n41\tSee: docs/error-handling-standardization.md for complete pattern comparison and examples.\n42\t\"\"\"\n43\t\n44\timport json\n45\tfrom logging import getLogger\n46\tfrom typing import Any, Dict, Optional, Union, cast\n47\t\n48\tlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n49\t\n50\t\n51\tclass ParseLLMError(Exception):\n52\t    \"\"\"\n53\t    Raised when LLM response parsing fails.\n54\t\n55\t    Attributes:\n56\t        message: Human-readable error message\n57\t        raw_response: The raw LLM response text that failed parsing (preserved for debugging)\n58\t\n59\t    The raw_response attribute is critical for debugging and degraded-state UX.\n60\t    Always pass it when raising ParseLLMError so error handlers can include\n61\t    response snippets in error events and logs.\n62\t    \"\"\"\n63\t\n64\t    def __init__(self, message: str, raw_response: Optional[str] = None):\n65\t        self.raw_response = raw_response\n66\t        super().__init__(message)\n67\t\n68\t\n69\tdef strip_markdown_fences(raw: str) -> str:\n70\t    \"\"\"\n71\t    Strip markdown code fences from a response using fast manual string splitting.\n72\t\n73\t    Handles:\n74\t    - ```json ... ```\n75\t    - ``` ... ```\n76\t    - ` ... ` (rare but possible)\n77\t\n78\t    Performance: This is the optimal approach\n80\t    for hot-path LLM response parsing.\n81\t\n82\t    Args:\n83\t        raw: Raw response text that may contain markdown fences\n84\t\n85\t    Returns:\n86\t        Text with markdown fences removed\n87\t\n88\t    Examples:\n89\t        >>> strip_markdown_fences('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n90\t        '{\"a\": 1}'\n91\t        >>> strip_markdown_fences('```\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n92\t        '{\"a\": 1}'\n93\t        >>> strip_markdown_fences('{\"a\": 1}')\n94\t        '{\"a\": 1}'\n95\t    \"\"\"\n96\t    # Fallback: try to stringify the content\n163\t    logger.warning(\"Could not extract text from content array, using fallback\")\n164\t    return str(content)\n165\t\n166\t\n167\tdef parse_llm_response(\n168\t    raw_text: str,\n169\t    *,\n170\t    strip_fences: bool = True,\n171\t    expect_json: bool = True,\n172\t) -> Union[str, Dict[str, Any]]:\n173\t    \"\"\"\n174\t    Parse an LLM text response, optionally stripping fences and parsing JSON.\n175\t\n176\t    This is the main entry point for parsing LLM responses across the codebase.\n177\t    It handles the common pattern of:\n178\t    1. Stripping markdown code fences (```json ... ```)\n179\t    2. Parsing JSON (when expect_json=True)\n180\t    3. Providing clear error messages\n181\t\n182\t    Performance: Optimized with fast manual fence stripping (7-179x faster than\n183\t    regex) and early returns on empty inputs. Operations are sequential but efficient.\n184\t\n185\t    Args:\n186\t        raw_text: Raw response text from LLM\n187\t        strip_fences: Whether to strip markdown code fences (default: True)\n188\t        expect_json: Whether to parse as JSON (default: True)\n189\t\n190\t    Returns:\n191\t        Parsed JSON object if expect_json=True, otherwise cleaned text\n192\t\n193\t    Raises:\n194\t        ParseLLMError: If parsing fails\n195\t\n196\t    Examples:\n197\t        >>> parse_llm_response('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n198\t        {'a': 1}\n199\t        >>> parse_llm_response('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```', expect_json=False)\n200\t        '{\"a\": 1}'\n201\t    \"\"\"\n202\t    # Centralized utility for parsing LLM responses.\n\nHandles:\n1. ZAI proxy response unwrapping (removes outer \"result\" field)\n2. Markdown code fence stripping (```json, ```, etc.)\n3. JSON parsing with clear error messages\n4. Graceful fallback for malformed responses\n\nThis utility is used across:\n- Intent Router (src/intent/router.py)\n- Synthesize Strand (src/synthesize/strand.py)\n- Self-Modification Agent (src/agents/self_modification.py)\n- Any other LLM interaction points\n\n## Error Handling Patterns\n\nWhen ParseLLMError is raised, choose recovery strategy based on context:\n\n### Corrective Retry Pattern (Router-style)\nUse when:\n- LLM call is early in pipeline (cheap to retry)\n- Failure cascades to downstream operations\n- Retrying doesn't require re-running expensive operations\n\nExample: Intent router retries once because malformed response affects all downstream processing.\n\nSee: src/intent/router.py classify_utterance()\n\n### Fallback Result Pattern (Synthesize-style)\nUse when:\n- LLM call is AFTER expensive operations (fetch, DB queries, etc.)\n- Data already obtained should not be discarded\n- User should see partial results in degraded-state UX\n\nExample: Synthesize strand returns fallback result because fetch operations already completed.\n\nSee: src/synthesize/strand.py synthesize()\n\n### Documentation\nSee: docs/error-handling-standardization.md for complete pattern comparison and examples.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport json\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom typing import Any, Dict, Optional, Union, cast\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n\nclass ParseLLMError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"\n    Raised when LLM response parsing fails.\n\n    Attributes:\n        message: Human-readable error message\n        raw_response: The raw LLM response text that failed parsing (preserved for debugging)\n\n    The raw_response attribute is critical for debugging and degraded-state UX.\n    Always pass it when raising ParseLLMError so error handlers can include\n    response snippets in error events and logs.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, message: str, raw_response: Optional[str] = None):\n        self.raw_response = raw_response\n        super().__init__(message)\n\n\ndef strip_markdown_fences(raw: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Strip markdown code fences from a response using fast manual string splitting.\n\n    Handles:\n    - ```json ... ```\n    - ``` ... ```\n    - ` ... ` (rare but possible)\n\n    Performance: Uses manual string splitting which is 7-179x faster than regex-based\n    alternatives (see benchmarking in notes/adc-3e5gg.md). This is the optimal approach\n    for hot-path LLM response parsing.\n\n    Args:\n        raw: Raw response text that may contain markdown fences\n\n    Returns:\n        Text with markdown fences removed\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> strip_markdown_fences('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n        >>> strip_markdown_fences('```\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n        >>> strip_markdown_fences('{\"a\": 1}')\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n    \"\"\"\n    # Early return for empty/whitespace strings\n    if not raw or not raw.strip():\n        return raw\n\n    # Optimized: single strip at start, avoid redundant operations\n    text = raw.strip()\n\n    # Fast manual fence stripping (7-179x faster than regex)\n    # Pattern: ```optional_lang\\n content \\n```\n    if text.startswith(\"```\"):\n        # Split after first newline to skip opening fence line\n        text = text.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n        # Remove closing fence and any trailing whitespace\n        text = text.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n\n    return text\n\n\ndef unwrap_zai_response(response_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Unwrap ZAI proxy response structure.\n\n    The ZAI proxy wraps Anthropic responses under a \"result\" key.\n    This function extracts the inner payload if present.\n\n    Args:\n        response_data: Raw response dict from ZAI proxy\n\n    Returns:\n        Unwrapped response dict (or original if no wrapping detected)\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> unwrap_zai_response({\"result\": {\"content\": [...]}})\n        {\"content\": [...]}\n        >>> unwrap_zai_response({\"content\": [...]})\n        {\"content\": [...]}\n    \"\"\"\n    # Fallback: try to stringify the content\n    logger.warning(\"Could not extract text from content array, using fallback\")\n    return str(content)\n\n\ndef parse_llm_response(\n    raw_text: str,\n    *,\n    strip_fences: bool = True,\n    expect_json: bool = True,\n) -> Union[str, Dict[str, Any]]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse an LLM text response, optionally stripping fences and parsing JSON.\n\n    This is the main entry point for parsing LLM responses across the codebase.\n    It handles the common pattern of:\n    1. Stripping markdown code fences (```json ... ```)\n    2. Parsing JSON (when expect_json=True)\n    3. Providing clear error messages\n\n    Performance: Optimized with fast manual fence stripping (7-179x faster than\n    regex) and early returns on empty inputs. Operations are sequential but efficient.\n\n    Args:\n        raw_text: Raw response text from LLM\n        strip_fences: Whether to strip markdown code fences (default: True)\n        expect_json: Whether to parse as JSON (default: True)\n\n    Returns:\n        Parsed JSON object if expect_json=True, otherwise cleaned text\n\n    Raises:\n        ParseLLMError: If parsing fails\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> parse_llm_response('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n        {'a': 1}\n        >>> parse_llm_response('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```', expect_json=False)\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n    \"\"\"\n    # Early return for empty input\n    if not raw_text or not raw_text.strip():\n        if expect_json:\n            raise ParseLLMError(\n                \"Empty response provided\",\n                raw_response=raw_text,\n            )\n        return raw_text\n\n    try:\n        # Step 1: Strip markdown fences if present\n        if strip_fences:\n            text = strip_markdown_fences(raw_text)\n        else:\n            text = raw_text.strip()\n\n        # Step 2: Parse JSON if expected\n        if expect_json:\n            try:\n                return json.loads(text)\n            except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n                # Provide helpful error message with snippet\n                snippet = text[:200] if text else \"(empty)\"\n                raise ParseLLMError(\n                    f\"Failed to parse JSON: {e}\\nResponse snippet: {snippet}...\",\n                    raw_response=raw_text,\n                ) from e\n\n        return text\n\n    except ParseLLMError:\n        # Re-raise ParseLLMError without wrapping\n        raise\n    except Exception as e:\n        raise ParseLLMError(\n            f\"Unexpected error parsing response: {e}\",\n            raw_response=raw_text,\n        ) from e\n\n\ndef parse_zai_proxy_response(\n    response_dict: Dict[str, Any],\n    *,\n    extract_text: bool = True,\n    strip_fences: bool = True,\n    expect_json: bool = True,\n) -> Union[str, Dict[str, Any]]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse a complete ZAI proxy response from HTTP.\n\n    This handles the full flow:\n    1. Fallback Result Pattern (Synthesize)\n- Catches `ParseLLMError` on malformed JSON\n- Returns fallback `SynthesizeResult` instead of raising\n- Preserves expensive fetch data (no re-fetch needed)\n- Enables degraded-state UX with partial results\n\n## Maintenance Going Forward\n\nTo maintain performance and correctness:\n\n1. **Before changing fence-stripping logic:**\n   -
